name: Picón del Buitre - Astrophysical Observatory of Javalambre – circular trail run from Arcos de las Salinas
name_original: Picón del Buitre - Observatorio Astrofísico de Javalambre – trail run circular desde Arcos de las Salinas
region_intro: Jogging around Arcos de las Salinas offers routes through diverse natural landscapes in the Gúdar-Javalambre region of Teruel, Spain. The area, situated at an altitude of 1081 meters, features mountains, extensive pine forests, and the Arcos River, which contributes to lush riparian environments. This Protected Area (LIC) provides varied terrain, from riverside paths to more demanding mountain trails, including ascents to peaks like Pico del Buitre. The region's geological features and historic salt flats also add to the scenic diversity for runners. Best jogging routes around Arcos De Las Salinas The most popular jogging route is Picón del Buitre - Astrophysical Observatory of Javalambre – circular trail run from Arcos de las Salinas, a 11.6 miles (18.7 km) trail that takes 2 hours 36 minutes to complete. This difficult route includes significant elevation gain and passes by the Javalambre Astrophysical Observatory. Another top favourite among local runners is Javalambre – trail run circular from Javalambre Ski Resort, a difficult 4.1 miles (6.6 km) path. This circular route explores the Javalambre area, starting from the ski resort. Local runners also love the Refugio Rabadá y Navarro – Pico Javalambre loop from Parking Estación Javalambre, a 15.8 miles (25.4 km) trail leading through the Pico Javalambre area, often completed in about 4 hours 27 minutes. Jogging around Arcos de las Salinas is defined by mountain trails, pine forests, and riverside paths. The network offers options for different ability levels, from gentle routes along the Arcos River to challenging ascents in the mountains. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 60 runners have used komoot to explore Arcos de las Salinas's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available around Arcos de las Salinas?
A: There are over 10 dedicated jogging routes around Arcos de las Salinas, offering a variety of experiences from riverside paths to challenging mountain trails. These routes cater to different fitness levels, with options for both moderate and difficult runs.

Q: Are there any easy or beginner-friendly running paths in Arcos de las Salinas?
A: While most routes in the guide are rated difficult due to the mountainous terrain, the region also offers gentler options. The &lt;a href="https://es.wikipedia.org/wiki/Arcos_de_las_Salinas" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Senda Fluvial Desiderio&lt;/a&gt; along the Arcos River is known for being a simple and beautiful walk, ideal for a more relaxed run. Additionally, the 4.6-kilometer route to the historic Royal Salt Flats has minimal elevation change, making it suitable for beginners.

Q: What kind of natural landscapes can I expect to see while jogging here?
A: Jogging around Arcos de las Salinas immerses you in diverse natural landscapes. You'll encounter extensive pine forests covering mountain slopes, lush riparian environments along the Arcos and Torrijano Rivers, and unique geological features like Las Torcas and Cerro Negro. Higher altitude routes offer panoramic views from peaks such as Pico del Buitre.

Q: Are there any circular running routes available?
A: Yes, several circular running routes are featured. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e983336692/picon-del-buitre-astrophysical-observatory-of-javalambre-circular-trail-run-from-arcos-de-las-salinas" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Picón del Buitre - Astrophysical Observatory of Javalambre – circular trail run from Arcos de las Salinas&lt;/a&gt; is a challenging 18.7 km loop. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e983277552/javalambre-trail-run-circular-from-javalambre-ski-resort" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Javalambre – trail run circular from Javalambre Ski Resort&lt;/a&gt;, which explores the Javalambre area.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the running trails?
A: Generally, trails in natural areas like Arcos de las Salinas are dog-friendly, especially if your dog is well-behaved and kept on a leash. It's always recommended to check local regulations for specific trails or protected areas, but the Senda Fluvial Desiderio and forest paths are typically suitable for running with dogs.

Q: What are some notable attractions or viewpoints along the running routes?
A: Many routes offer stunning sights. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e983336692/picon-del-buitre-astrophysical-observatory-of-javalambre-circular-trail-run-from-arcos-de-las-salinas" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Picón del Buitre trail&lt;/a&gt; leads past the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2305519" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Javalambre Astrophysical Observatory&lt;/a&gt;, offering impressive views. The Senda Fluvial Desiderio features two recreational areas and a small waterfall. You can also explore the historic &lt;a href="https://es.wikipedia.org/wiki/Arcos_de_las_Salinas" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salinas de Arcos&lt;/a&gt; (salt flats) and pass by the Fuente del Molino (Mill Spring) and Ermita de la Virgen de los Dolores on a 4.6 km route.

Q: Is there parking available near the trailheads?
A: Yes, parking is generally available. For routes exploring the Javalambre area, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20138236" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Refugio Rabadá y Navarro – Pico Javalambre loop&lt;/a&gt;, you can find parking at the Estación Javalambre. For routes closer to the village, like the Senda Fluvial Desiderio, parking can often be found near the starting points or within Arcos de las Salinas itself.

Q: What is the best time of year for running in Arcos de las Salinas?
A: The region offers pleasant running conditions across different seasons. Autumn is particularly enjoyable for riverside paths like the Senda Fluvial Desiderio, with vibrant foliage. Spring brings mild temperatures and blooming landscapes. Summer can be warm, so early morning or late afternoon runs are advisable, especially on exposed mountain trails. Winter offers unique challenges and snowy landscapes at higher elevations.

Q: Are there any routes suitable for families with children?
A: For families, the Senda Fluvial Desiderio along the Arcos River is an excellent choice. It's a simple and beautiful path with minimal elevation, featuring recreational areas that can be enjoyed by all ages. The 4.6-kilometer route to the Royal Salt Flats is also relatively flat and offers historical interest, making it a good option for a family outing.

Q: What do other runners say about the trails in Arcos de las Salinas?
A: More than 60 runners have used komoot to explore Arcos de las Salinas's varied terrain. They often praise the region's diverse landscapes, from tranquil riverside paths to challenging mountain ascents, and the opportunity to experience both natural beauty and historical sites. The routes are appreciated for their natural feel and the sense of adventure they provide.

Q: Are there any waterfalls to see on the running routes?
A: Yes, the &lt;a href="https://es.wikipedia.org/wiki/Arcos_de_las_Salinas" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Senda Fluvial Desiderio&lt;/a&gt;, which runs alongside the Arcos River, features a small waterfall at its end, offering a refreshing and scenic spot. Additionally, the nearby &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4457070" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Amanaderos of Riodeva&lt;/a&gt; is a notable waterfall in the broader region that can be explored.

Q: What is the typical difficulty level of running routes in this area?
A: The running routes around Arcos de las Salinas offer a range of difficulties. While some paths along the river are relatively easy, many of the routes, especially those venturing into the mountains, are rated as difficult. These challenging trails often involve significant elevation gain, such as the 936 meters of ascent on the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e983336692/picon-del-buitre-astrophysical-observatory-of-javalambre-circular-trail-run-from-arcos-de-las-salinas" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Picón del Buitre circular trail&lt;/a&gt;, making them suitable for experienced runners seeking a demanding workout.
tips: If possible, mount a light development to climb without overloading it, it is quite a hard climb. Bring water and avoid the hottest hours in summer, because in the upper part there are no shadows
